meta.line1: Agreement on the use of the TyPlace marketplace (public offer).

note.text1: TyPlace is a platform where users conclude transactions with each other. TyPlace does not sell goods on its own behalf and is not a party to transactions, but provides tools including escrow (secure transaction), chat, and support.

defs.title: 1. Terms and Definitions
defs.siteText: the TyPlace online platform (including website, mobile versions, APIs, interfaces).
defs.operatorText1: the entity that manages the Platform and ensures its operation (details are published on the page
defs.sellerText: a User who posts listings and offers digital goods/services.
defs.digitalText: intangible goods/services intended for use in a digital environment (games, accounts, in-game items/currency, digital services, etc.).
defs.adText: a Seller’s publication describing the offer, price, terms, and deadlines.
defs.dealText: an agreement between the Seller and the Buyer regarding a specific listing.
defs.escrowText: a mechanism for holding the Buyer’s funds until confirmation of transaction completion.
defs.balanceText: a virtual record of funds within the Platform (not a bank account).

accept.title: 2. Acceptance of Terms
accept.p1: 2.1. This Agreement constitutes a public offer. Registration, account login, posting listings, or using Platform functions signifies full and unconditional acceptance of these terms.
accept.p2: 2.2. If the User does not agree with the terms, they must discontinue using the Platform.

eligibility.title: 3. Age and Legal Capacity
eligibility.p1: 3.1. Purchase/sale functions and payment processing are available to individuals with sufficient legal capacity to enter into transactions.
eligibility.p2: 3.2. The service is intended for individuals aged 18+. If the laws of the User’s country require a different age or parental/guardian consent, the User agrees to comply with such requirements.

account.title: 4. Account
account.p1: 4.1. Registration is required to access extended features.
account.p2: 4.2. The User must keep login credentials confidential. All actions performed from the User’s account are deemed performed by the User.
account.p3: 4.3. It is prohibited to transfer accounts to third parties, use other users’ accounts, or create accounts to bypass restrictions.
account.p4: 4.4. The Operator may request proof of account ownership in case of suspected compromise.

platform.title: 5. Platform Status
platform.p1: 5.1. TyPlace acts as an informational intermediary and technological platform providing Users with tools to interact and conclude transactions.
platform.p2: 5.2. The Operator is not a seller of digital goods/services, does not become a party to transactions between Users, and does not acquire ownership of transaction items.
platform.p3: 5.3. The Operator may provide service functions (escrow, support, rules, anti-fraud) and charge a service fee/commission.

listings.title: 6. Listings (Seller Content)
listings.p1: 6.1. The Seller independently posts listings and is responsible for their content.
listings.p2: 6.2. The Seller guarantees they have the right to offer the goods/services and that the listing does not infringe third-party rights.
listings.p3: 6.3. The Seller undertakes to specify accurate terms: composition of goods/services, limitations, deadlines, and Buyer requirements.
listings.p4: 6.4. The Operator may hide or remove listings in case of rule violations, suspected fraud, or legal requirements.

prohibited.title: 7. Prohibited Actions and Content
prohibited.l1: posting illegal goods/services or content infringing third-party rights;
prohibited.l2: fraud, deception, misleading information, substitution of transaction subject;
prohibited.l3: bypassing escrow or commission, encouraging off-platform payments, exchanging contacts to bypass rules;
prohibited.l4: sharing card or banking details in chats;
prohibited.l5: malware, hacking data, phishing, exploitation of vulnerabilities;
prohibited.l6: threats, harassment, hate speech, prohibited content;
prohibited.l7: violating rules of specific games/services if this makes the transaction illegal or prohibited.
prohibited.p2: 7.2. The Operator may apply measures (restrictions, suspension, freezing payouts) in case of violations.

deal.title: 8. Transaction and Communication
deal.p1: 8.1. Transactions are concluded directly between Seller and Buyer through the Platform interface.
deal.p2: 8.2. The Platform chat is used to discuss terms. This helps document agreements and evidence.
deal.p3: 8.3. Users must act in good faith: avoid delays, abuse of disputes, or manipulation of reviews.

escrow.title: 9. Escrow (Secure Transaction)
escrow.p1: 9.1. When escrow is used, Buyer’s funds are held until transaction completion.
escrow.p2: 9.2. Payment to the Seller is made after confirmation of completion or dispute resolution.
escrow.p3: 9.3. The Operator may temporarily hold funds in case of suspected fraud, chargeback, or rule violations.
escrow.p4: 9.4. Escrow reduces risks but does not guarantee absolute protection.

fees.title: 10. Fees and Charges
fees.p1: 10.1. The Platform may charge a service fee (commission) for facilitating transactions.
fees.p2: 10.2. The commission may vary depending on category, country, or payment method.
fees.p3: 10.3. Additional payment provider fees may apply.
fees.p4: 10.4. Use of the Platform constitutes acceptance of the current terms.

withdrawals.title: 11. Payouts and Withdrawals
withdrawals.p1: 11.1. The Seller gains access to funds after completion of transactions and necessary checks.
withdrawals.p2: 11.2. The Operator may set technical limits and delays for withdrawals.
withdrawals.p3: 11.3. The User is responsible for the accuracy of payment details.

refunds.title: 12. Cancellations, Refunds and Chargebacks
refunds.p1: 12.1. A transaction may be cancelled by mutual agreement of the parties or through support.
refunds.p2: 12.2. After confirmation of fulfillment, refunds may not be possible for digital goods.
refunds.p3: 12.3. In case of a chargeback, the Operator may temporarily freeze funds.

disputes.title: 13. Disputes and Support
disputes.p1: 13.1. In the event of a dispute, the User must contact support and provide evidence.
disputes.p2: 13.2. The Operator reviews the dispute and may decide on the allocation of funds.
disputes.p3: 13.3. Users agree to cooperate with support.

moderation.title: 14. Moderation, Suspensions, Measures
moderation.l4: cancel transactions in case of violations;
moderation.p2: 14.2. Measures are applied to protect users and prevent fraud.

ip.title: 15. Intellectual Property
ip.p1: 15.1. All rights to the design, code, and TyPlace brand belong to the Operator.
ip.p2: 15.2. The User grants the right to publish listing content on the Platform.
ip.p3: 15.3. It is prohibited to copy Platform elements or extract data without permission.

privacy.title: 16. Personal Data
privacy.p1: 16.1. Personal data is processed in accordance with the Privacy Policy published on the Platform.
privacy.p2: 16.2. The User agrees to data processing for contract performance, security, anti-fraud, support, and compliance with legal requirements.

aml.title: 17. AML/KYC, Verification and Security
aml.p1: 17.1. The Operator may conduct checks of transactions and accounts and request documents to comply with provider requirements and prevent fraud.
aml.p2: 17.2. If information is not provided, the Operator may restrict functions, suspend withdrawals, or terminate the agreement.
aml.p3: 17.3. The Operator may use automated risk analysis and anti-fraud systems.

international.title: 18. International Nature
international.p1: 18.1. The Platform is available to users from different countries. The User is responsible for compliance with the laws of their jurisdiction.
international.p2: 18.2. If the use of the Platform is prohibited by the laws of the User’s country, the User must refrain from using the service.

liability.title: 19. Limitation of Liability
liability.p1: 19.1. The Platform is provided on an "as is" basis. The Operator does not guarantee uninterrupted service.
liability.p2: 19.2. The Operator is not responsible for Users’ actions or the fulfillment of obligations between transaction parties.
liability.p3: 19.3. The Operator’s liability is limited to the amount of the service fee actually received for the disputed transaction.
liability.p4: 19.4. The Operator is not liable for indirect damages, lost profits, or reputational losses.

force.title: 20. Force Majeure
force.p1: 20.1. The parties are released from liability for failure to perform obligations due to force majeure circumstances.

changes.title: 21. Changes to Terms
changes.p1: 21.1. The Operator may amend the Agreement by publishing a new version on the Platform.
changes.p2: 21.2. Continued use of the Platform constitutes acceptance of the updated version.

law.title: 22. Governing Law and Claims Procedure
law.p1: 22.1. The Agreement is governed by the laws of the Operator’s country of registration.
law.p2: 22.2. At the time of publication, the Operator is registered in Ukraine.
law.p3: 22.3. Claims must be submitted to support or the legal email address.

final.title: 23. Final Provisions
final.p1: 23.1. If any provision is declared invalid, the remaining provisions remain in force.
final.p2: 23.2. The Operator may assign rights and obligations as part of a business transfer.
final.p3: 23.3. The User confirms that they have read and accepted these terms.
